    Signal ChainSession Template

Studio Workflow

Session Template

A recommended Ableton Live session template tailored to this studio's hardware. Save it as a Default Set so every new project starts configured and ready.

Signal Chain

MIDI Tracks

Track nameMIDI FromMIDI ToNotes
Hydra MIDIHydrasynth Explorer (USB)No Output (or External Instrument → MOTU Out)Capture Hydrasynth note data; External Instrument device routes audio back in on Inputs 3/4
Piano MIDIP-225 (USB-B)No Output (or soft synth)Use P-225 as MIDI controller for NI Kontakt, Addictive Keys, etc.
Launchkey MIDILaunchkey 37 Mk3 (USB)All instruments via racks or routingScale Mode active; Chord Mode optional
Seaboard MPESeaboard M (USB)MPE-enabled instrument rackEnable MPE in track's MIDI input — use MPE Control M4L device for non-MPE targets

Audio Tracks

Track nameAudio FromChannelDefault device chain
VocalExt. In1 (Mono)EQ Eight → Compressor → De-esser; sends to Verb and Delay returns
VT-3 FXExt. In2 (Mono)EQ Eight; blend against clean Vocal track
Hydra AudioExt. In3/4 (Stereo)EQ Eight; send to Verb return
Piano AudioExt. In5/6 (Stereo)EQ Eight; optional light compression

Return Tracks

  • Return A — Reverb: Valhalla Supermassive (or Live's Reverb) — long, lush tail. All instruments send here; vocal send lower than synths.
  • Return B — Delay: Ping Pong Delay — tempo-synced 1/8 or 1/4 note. Vocal send for slapback; Hydrasynth send for spacious pads.
  • Return C — Vinyl Sim: Vinyl Distortion M4L or Redux — optional lo-fi bus. Send Hydrasynth or Piano for degraded texture layers.

Master Track

  • Glue Compressor: subtle bus glue — ratio 2:1, attack 30 ms, release auto, threshold just catching peaks, 1–2 dB GR.
  • EQ Eight: high-pass at 30 Hz to cut sub rumble; gentle shelf adjustments only for reference checking.
  • Loudness Meter M4L (optional): monitor LUFS-I during mixdown; target −14 LUFS-I for streaming delivery.

Push 1 Setup

  • Push 1 automatically reflects whichever track is selected in Live.
  • Pre-assign Macro 1–4 on key Instrument Racks to the most-used sound-shaping parameters (e.g., Filter Cutoff, Resonance, Reverb Send, Envelope Release).
  • Save clip names in the Session View grid before performance: rows A–C = intro / verse / chorus variations per instrument column.
  • Set Push metronome and tempo in the template to your usual working BPM (e.g., 120 BPM).

Knobbler Assignment

  • Knobs 1–4 → Hydrasynth Macro 1–4 (via MIDI Map Mode in Ableton: assign to External Instrument device macro parameters).
  • Knobs 5–6 → Reverb Return send level (Vocal / Hydra).
  • Knobs 7–8 → Master EQ treble and bass shelves for quick reference adjustment.

Saving as Default Set

  1. Build and confirm the template loads without errors (all inputs present, no missing devices).
  2. In Ableton: File → Save Live Set As Template.
  3. Name it Studio Template — [date] and save to your User Library Templates folder.
  4. Go to Preferences → File/Folder → Save Current Set as Default so every File → New uses this layout.

Built for fast lookups, cleaner routing decisions, and practical studio recall.
